bayou_city_bistro_recommend.final_response:
I have successfully analyzed the delivery requirements for **Bayou City Bistro**.

### Decision Summary
The recommended assignment is **RTE-4100 - Central Houston** on **Tuesday** in the **07:14-10:14** window.

### Primary Reasons
1. **Geographic fit:** This route averages 1.7 miles to existing stops, providing excellent density.
2. **Capacity headroom:** The truck will be at approximately 58% capacity after adding this order, leaving 440 cases of headroom, which is well within the 75% comfort threshold.
3. **Preferred-window match:** The assigned window covers 166 of the 180 preferred minutes for Tuesday morning.
4. **Slot openness:** The tier-weighted contention for this slot is 2.80 with an openness of 0.26.

### Key Trade-off
This choice edges out the runner-up, **RTE-4110 - Downtown / Midtown**, on overall route-slot score (0.80 vs 0.59). By choosing the Central Houston route, we are able to accommodate the customer's preferred day (Tuesday) while also leading or matching the runner-up on all scored factors.

The third candidate, **RTE-4200 - West Houston / Energy Corridor**, was found to be infeasible due to service area constraints.

galleria_grill_escalate_low_score.final_response:
SITUATION
Galleria Grill & Catering (400 cases) requires a route assignment as no slot reached the auto-assign threshold.

ROOT CAUSE
No route met the auto-assign score threshold; the highest score was 0.40 on RTE-4200 due to constraints on geographic clustering and slot availability.

OPTIONS (most workable first)
1) RTE-4200 - West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED — 84% utilization with 150 cases of headroom.
   Action: Assign to the Wednesday 11:30-14:30 window.
   Trade-off: Higher mileage per stop (8.2 mi) compared to available alternatives.
2) RTE-4100 - Central Houston · TUE — 88% utilization with 130 cases of headroom.
   Action: Assign to the Tuesday 10:00-13:00 window.
   Trade-off: Tighter capacity buffer and higher utilization compared to RTE-4200.

RECOMMENDATION
Start with RTE-4200 - West Houston / Energy Corridor as it provides the largest capacity buffer (150 cases of headroom) and lower post-assignment utilization (84%).

DECISION NEEDED
Do you approve the assignment of Galleria Grill & Catering to RTE-4200 on Wednesday, or prefer the tighter capacity utilization of RTE-4100 on Tuesday?

katy_prairie_escalate_out_of_range.final_response:

woodlands_fresh_cafe_recommend.final_response:
I have processed the request for Woodlands Fresh Cafe.

**Decision Summary:**
I recommend assigning the customer to **RTE-4300 - North Houston / The Woodlands** on **Thursday** with a delivery window of **08:30-11:30**.

**Primary Reasons:**
1. **Geographic fit:** The location is highly efficient, with an average of 1.3 miles to existing stops.
2. **Capacity headroom:** The truck will be approximately 81% full after this order, leaving 150 cases of headroom.
3. **Preferred-window match:** The recommended 08:30-11:30 window covers 150 of the 180 minutes requested for Thursday.
4. **Slot openness:** There is manageable contention for this time slot (tier-weighted contention of 2.70 with 4 existing stops overlapping).

**Key Trade-off:**
This assignment edges out the runner-up, **RTE-4310 - Spring / South Woodlands** (Friday), on overall route-slot score (0.69 vs 0.55). While the runner-up offers slightly more capacity headroom (0.83 vs 0.58), the recommended route is a better fit overall and aligns with the customer's preferred delivery day.
